question_text: Explain why ultraviolet radiation can damage skin but visible light cannot.
correct_answer: Ultraviolet radiation has a shorter wavelength and higher frequency than visible light, so it carries more energy. This energy can damage skin cells and cause sunburn.
explanation: In the electromagnetic spectrum, UV waves have more energy than visible light waves. This extra energy can harm the molecules in our skin and cause damage. Visible light doesn't have enough energy to cause this damage.
